title: Should pajamas be banned at Logan Airport? Here’s what readers said.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: 48% of Boston.com readers support banning pajamas at Logan Airport, while 42% oppose, and 11% are conditional.
Tampa International Airport's social media joke about banning pajamas sparked national debate on airport dress code.
U.S. Department of Transportation campaign promotes 'civility' in travel, questioning appropriateness of wearing pajamas in public.

title: ‘A crisis of Trump’s creation’: Mass. politicians respond to strikes on Iran
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts politicians condemned President Trump's authorization of strikes on Iran, calling the actions illegal and demanding congressional debate on war powers.
Sen. Ed Markey and Sen. Elizabeth Warren criticized Trump, emphasizing diplomacy and addressing domestic issues as priorities over military action.
Rep. Seth Moulton and Rep. Ayanna Pressley highlighted concerns about lack of strategy and civilian casualties, urging Congress to limit presidential war powers.

title: Carmelo Anthony, Tracy McGrady impressed with Celtics after win vs. 76ers
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Boston Celtics defeated the Philadelphia 76ers 114-98 on March 1, impressing analysts Carmelo Anthony and Tracy McGrady.
Celtics center Neemias Queta tied Jaylen Brown with 27 points, contributing significantly to the team’s second-half performance.
Analysts highlighted the Celtics' effective strategy, noting their defensive energy and fast-paced, three-point shooting style.

title: Fatal fire under investigation in Roxbury
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A person was found dead in a fatal fire on Ellingwood Street in Roxbury on Friday afternoon.
Boston Police and Fire Departments are investigating the cause of the fire that occurred in the residential building.
The fire started around 3:00 p.m., affecting all three floors, and remains under active investigation by authorities.

title: 5 restaurants we can’t wait to try in March
author: Jillian Dara
bullets: Bambola opening in Seaport offers classic Roman and Southern Italian cuisine with a vintage-supper club ambiance late March 2026.
Common Craft expands to South Boston with chef Tony Messina, featuring themed dining and a speakeasy opening mid-March 2026.
MEZŌ Mediterranean brings Greek dishes to Dedham Square with a focus on shared plates, opening March 4, 2026.

title: What Boston meteorologists are saying about Tuesday’s ‘wintry mix’
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Boston meteorologists predict snow, sleet, and freezing rain could impact Tuesday's travel.
Temperatures will climb into the mid-upper 30s, changing snow to rain by Tuesday evening.
Colder start to the week with milder temperatures expected by the weekend reaching into the 50s.

title: Neemias Queta played one of his best games as Celtics pull away late vs. 76ers: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Neemias Queta excelled with 27 points and 17 rebounds, crucial in the Celtics' 114-98 win over the 76ers.
Jaylen Brown contributed 27 points, 8 rebounds, and 8 assists, showcasing improved passing skills against Philadelphia.
Celtics center Nikola Vucevic added 11 points and 12 rebounds, showing strengths and weaknesses against the Sixers.

title: Parole rate is up in Mass., including for people with life sentences
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Massachusetts parole rate rose by 3.5% in 2024, influenced by the Commonwealth v. Mattis court decision.
Life sentence parole rate increased to 72% in 2024 from 57% in 2023, reflecting a 15% rise.
1,280 individuals were on parole in 2024, with 400 serving life sentences, up from 1,176 in 2023.

title: New photo released in unsolved 1997 homicide of a N.H. woman
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A new photo of victim Rosalie Miller has been released in the unsolved 1997 homicide case in New Hampshire.
Miller, 36, was last seen in December 1996 and found dead by ligature strangulation in January 1997 in Auburn.
Authorities urge anyone with information from 1996 near Londonderry Turnpike to contact the New Hampshire Cold Case Unit.

title: Man allegedly kills man in R.I. before causing car crash that killed 2 in Mass.
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Demitri Sousa, 28, is charged with murder after allegedly shooting Javon Lawson, 35, in Cranston, Rhode Island.
Sousa allegedly fled in a white Infiniti sedan, later causing a fatal crash that killed two in Swansea, Massachusetts.
Police found a pistol and ammunition in Sousa's car; he's held in custody pending medical clearance at Rhode Island Hospital.
